_note: Single source of truth for Ansu boons (there are no banes — the system's teeth are control, not prices). Names and descriptions are authored in English (GM directive). Number tokens: {{ansuReleaseDC}}, {{ansuTempHp}}, {{ansuResist}}, {{ansuLevel}}, {{ansuDuration}} — baked at compose time and recomputed on attunement or character-level change.
_designNote: GM-approved ladder (2026-07-06): rev-2 artifact text verbatim. `always: true` = Inheritance (persistent knowledge, lives on the Attunement marker); everything else lives on the Communion effect and exists only while it runs. Gate `subjugated` = Mastery-only. The metal is Salbarium (Сальбарий).

entries.name: Invoke the Ansu
entries.description:
You stop holding the door shut. The presence in your blood floods through you: you gain the Communion benefits of your attunement rank.

Duration {{ansuDuration}} (1 round at Trial, 3 rounds at Discipline, 1 minute at Union). When Communion ends — or when you try to end it early — attempt a Will save against the Release DC ({{ansuReleaseDC}}).

You can Invoke at will, but not while Communion is already running.

entries.name: Release the Ansu
entries.description:
You loosen your grip on the borrowed strength and try to hand it back. Attempt a Will save against the Release DC ({{ansuReleaseDC}}).

  • Critical Success Clean release; the discipline feeds the Climb (+2).
  • Success Clean release (+1 to the Climb).
  • Failure The Ansu doesn't let go: Lingering — the boons stay on, the Ansu steers. Re-save at the end of each of your turns.
  • Critical Failure Seizure — the Ansu takes the body for 1 round (the GM plays you), then Lingering resumes.

entries.name: Ancestral Vigor
entries.description: The Ansu's strength pours into muscle and bone. When you Invoke the Ansu you gain {{ansuTempHp}} temporary Hit Points, and you gain a +1 status bonus to melee damage rolls while Communion lasts.

entries.name: Bull's Footing
entries.description: Hooves strike the ground like hammers on an anvil. You gain a +10-foot status bonus to your Speed, and when you Stride in a straight line toward an enemy you ignore non-magical difficult terrain.

rules.title: Bull's Footing
rules.text: Striding in a straight line toward an enemy: ignore non-magical difficult terrain.

entries.name: Tongue of the Makers
entries.description:
The Ansu murmurs the old words until they are yours. You can speak, read and write Murkhor and recognize the maker-glyphs of your people. You gain a +1 circumstance bonus to Decipher Writing and to Recall Knowledge about murkhor works, ruins and devices.

This knowledge stays even while the Ansu sleeps.

rules.title: Tongue of the Makers
rules.text: Knows Murkhor. +1 circumstance to Decipher Writing and Recall Knowledge about murkhor works.

entries.name: Call the Blade
entries.description:
You remember where your weapon is the way you remember where your hand is. One weapon you own flies to your open grasp from up to 60 feet — walls and physical barriers don't block the call, though sealed magical wards do. If a creature is holding it, the weapon strains but doesn't tear free.

(The Ansu once called Афина through the walls of a fighting club. This is that, learned.)

entries.name: Horns Reforged
entries.description: What the Ansu broke, the Ansu rebuilds — while it's close. During Communion your shattered horns knit into whole ridged weapons: you gain a horns unarmed Strike (1d8 piercing). When Communion ends, they crumble back to stumps.

entries.name: Salbarine Skin
entries.description: A pale metal sheen spreads over hide and scar — the same gleam as the Ansu's ornaments. You gain resistance {{ansuResist}} to physical damage (2 at attunement 5, 3 at 7, 4 at 10).

entries.name: Engineer's Eye
entries.description:
You begin to see the world the way your people built it: as mechanism. You gain a +1 circumstance bonus to Recall Knowledge about constructs, traps and mechanisms, and to Crafting checks to Disable a Device on mechanical devices.

This knowledge stays even while the Ansu sleeps.

rules.title: Engineer's Eye
rules.text: +1 circumstance to Recall Knowledge about constructs, traps and mechanisms.

entries.name: Overwhelm
entries.description:
One blow, the way the Ansu fights. Make a melee Strike. If it hits, immediately attempt an Athletics check to Shove or Trip the target; this check doesn't count toward your multiple attack penalty, and the Ansu's mass is behind it — +1 circumstance bonus.

(This is the arena: the construct's head, Стаф and Иримэ each dropped by a single hit.)

entries.name: Ancestral Vigor (Union)
entries.description: The Ansu's strength pours into muscle and bone. When you Invoke the Ansu you gain {{ansuTempHp}} temporary Hit Points, and you gain a +2 status bonus to melee damage rolls while Communion lasts.

entries.name: Ansu's Stature
entries.description: For a minute at a time, you are the thing that stood over you in your mind. When you Invoke the Ansu you grow to Large size: your reach increases by 5 feet, and your gear grows with you. Your Ancestral Vigor bonus improves to +2.

entries.name: Roar of the Old Blood
entries.description:
A sound out of a dead civilization's pride. Attempt an Intimidation check to Demoralize each enemy within 30 feet. You don't take the penalty for not sharing a language — fear this old needs no translation.

Once per Communion.

entries.name: Mind Like a Forge
entries.description: The Ansu drives the body faster than deliberation. While Communion lasts you are quickened; you can use the extra action only to Stride or Strike.

rules.title: Mind Like a Forge
rules.text: Quickened: the extra action is only for Stride or Strike.

entries.name: Permanent Communion
entries.description: There is no longer a door to hold shut — there is only you, larger inside than before. Communion no longer expires and never requires a Release save. You can enter or end it as a free action once per turn. Every Communion boon and Inheritance is simply yours now.

entries.name: Hand of the Ansu
entries.description: The mind-summons perfected. You Interact with an object at a distance — up to 120 feet, through walls and barriers: seize it, drag it to your hand, shove it, turn a wheel, throw a bolt. Call the Blade now works anywhere on the scene, no range limit. Creatures holding an object still keep their grip.
